title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: For questions 1–8, read the text below and choose which answer (A, B, C or D) best completes each gap.

content.text: The internet has (0) the way we live, work, and communicate. Its origins can be (1) back to the 1960s when the US military developed a network called ARPANET. This early system was designed to (2) a nuclear attack by allowing information to flow through multiple routes. Throughout the 1970s and 1980s, researchers (3) on this foundation, developing new protocols that would eventually become the basis of the modern internet. The World Wide Web, invented by Tim Berners-Lee in 1989, (4) the way for the explosion of online content we see today. By the mid-1990s, commercial websites had begun to (5) off, and millions of people were going online for the first time. The internet has since (6) rise to social media, e-commerce, and countless other innovations. However, it has also (7) concerns about privacy and security. Despite these challenges, the internet continues to evolve, and experts predict it will (8) an even greater role in our lives in the future.

example.explanation: 'Transformed' indica un cambio profundo y fundamental en algo.

questions.explanation: 'Traced back to' is the correct expression to trace the origin of something.
'Withstand' means to endure or resist something harmful.
'Built on' (with 'on' already in the text) means to develop something based on previous work.
'Paved the way for' (with 'the way for' already in the text) is the idiom for facilitating something future.
'Take off' (with 'off' already in the text) means to suddenly take off or succeed.
'Given rise to' (with 'rise to' already in the text) means to cause or provoke the appearance of something.
'Raised concerns' is the correct collocation for expressing concerns.
'Play a role' is the fixed expression for having importance in something.
